title: An Oath, a Map, and a Sending Stone
summary: No blade was drawn. The company took Azlund's oath under zone of truth, received the map of Chult and fifty gold apiece, and boarded The Horizon at the Waterdeep docks.
silent_roll: No blade was drawn; only a name was pressed into a sending stone.

title: The Horizon, and the First Storm
summary: Days aboard the Horizon: Chumble hid in a pickle-barrel and woke with a familiar named Wickles. The storm came on the fourth day; six hands met it, and every rope held.
silent_roll: A familiar was pulled from a pickle-barrel, and the company called him Wickles.

title: Blades in the Salt
summary: Sahuagin boarded at dusk. Corwin and Dragonbait held the prow; the company took the stern. Four fell to the party — blade, mockery, and bell. A flash of blue light behind a crate was not commented upon.

title: The Ring, Revealed
summary: An arm-wrestle unmasked no secrets, but words did. Harlen was revealed a Zhentarim stowaway and the cheese-thief. Corwin showed his left hand, told his name, and named the Ring of Winter.
silent_roll: A ring opened its eye in the galley, and a cheese-thief was revealed a spy.

title: Through Pirate Steel, into Nyanzaru
summary: Pirates boarded in the night. The deck ran red. When it became clear the company could not win the tally, Artus Cimber teleported them to Port Nyanzaru in a flash of ice and turned alone to the remaining crew.

title: Three Riders and a Ring
summary: The party rented three dinosaurs from the Tiryki Anchorage pens, crossed paths with Volo Geddarm on the march to Goldenthrone where they heard the history of Chult and bought his book, and ran the city race circuit — Lilac Mist took first place, and Greig finished third to claim Ubtao's Ring.
silent_roll: Half a goat was bought for five silver before the start; Scarback failed the last trench regardless.
A cage was opened mid-race, and the monkey chose the winner's shoulder over the open sky.
Tej was taken from a Grand Souk vendor at a full gallop, and the crowd roared.

title: Bones on the Steps of Tymora
summary: The party gathered intelligence from the innkeeper and Wakanga O'tamu—who gave Urida a journal and a task—then contracted Musharib as guide to Hrakhamar before cutting down a wave of undead attacking the Temple of Tymora in Malar's Throat.
silent_roll: A journal was placed in Urida's hands before any price was named.
The faces on the bulletin board were not all strangers.
Musharib found them unimpressive, and led them anyway.

title: The Stranger Spoke Once, Then Slept
summary: The party spent two days in Port Nyanzaru provisioning and gathering intelligence—visiting Grandfather Zitembe twice for visions and a briefing on the merchant princes, taking on Inete and Undril Silvertusk as companions bound for the jungle—before a stranger collapsed against Chumble in the market, whispered of the wise guardian of Orolunga, and woke the following morning with no memory of having spoken.
silent_roll: Three armed men were at Zitembe's door when the party arrived; no blood was drawn.
The message reached one ear alone; Chumble waited until they were safe before sharing it.
The man woke clean, the words he had carried gone from him entirely.

title: Down the Soshenstar by Canoe
summary: The party departed Port Nyanzaru on the fifth day, traveled the beach under Musharib's guidance, fended off two waves of flying monkeys, navigated a detour down a tributary where they recovered a magic ring from a Tyrannosaurus Zombie using Wickles as a lasso-bearer, and arrived one hex from Fort Righteous on the eleventh day with water supplies replenished.
silent_roll: A tabaxi minstrel returned double what she had borrowed, and Lilac gave one gold back.
Lilac spoke to brontosauruses, and the herd warned of undead nearby.
Chumble ate the dancing monkey fruit deliberately, and Undril spent a spell to undo the lesson.

title: The Shrine Yields Its Jug
summary: The party braved the trapped shrine at Camp Righteous, weathering repeated explosions and falls to retrieve a brown ceramic jug, then turned back a goblin ambush led by Yokka, sparing the boss and her two surviving minions in exchange for intelligence on undead and a rival goblin tribe to the north.
silent_roll: Yokka pulled a minion into the killing blow to save herself, and it worked.
Vex crossed the tile floor three times before going down; the door opened on the fourth try, and he didn't take the last step.
Chumble dreamed of something the others were not told.
